exemplar.title: Are we too quick to assume that the most recent evidence is inevitably the strongest? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Are we too quick to assume that the most recent evidence is inevitably the strongest? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained and explicit focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay
Effectively links both the human sciences and natural sciences to the core issue, using concrete, wellâchosen examples (the Hitler Diaries, the War of the Currents)
Presents clear, coherent lines of argument about differing attitudes toward new evidence in each area of knowledge
Demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives and touches on the implications of these viewpoints
weaknesses: Analysis remains at times partly descriptive and assertive rather than fully analytical
Key concepts (e.g. âcognitive biasâ, âpositivismâ) are introduced but not critically interrogated in depth
Reliance on a single main example per area of knowledge limits the breadth of support
Evaluation of counterclaims and deeper implications is only briefly addressed, lacking sustained depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Awarding 7 marks aligns with the examinerâs final total while reflecting the essayâs position between the 5â6 and 7â8 bands. The submission shows a sustained focus, clear and coherent arguments, effective use of specific examples, and some evaluation of different viewpoints. However, it falls short of the fully developed analytical depth, conceptual interrogation, and breadth of examples required to move firmly into the higher band. A score of 7 recognizes its strengths in focus and structure, while acknowledging the remaining limitations in depth and critical evaluation.

annotations.comment: Claims about delayed recognition of new evidence lack exploration of wider implications. The student should analyze potential risks and benefits of slower adoption in contexts such as public health or technology.

Assertions about qualitative data privileging in human sciences require deeper TOK reasoning. The student should critically examine why qualitative methods might amplify recency bias compared to quantitative approaches.

Using the War of the Currents case vividly demonstrates how skepticism of new evidence functions in natural sciences and contrasts with the human sciences example, reinforcing the comparative structure.

The student does not explore why Teslaâs AC eventually overcame DCâthis analysis would clarify how evidence strength shifts over time, directly linking to the essayâs focus on recency versus reliability.

The introduction asserts a thesis but does not clearly specify how recency bias differs between the two AoKs. The student should refine the thesis to explicitly outline the comparative argument linking recency and evidential strength for each AoK.

The method of analysis is not defined. The student should explain how examples will be evaluated using TOK concepts (e.g., cognitive bias, authority) to provide a consistent analytical framework throughout the essay.

The discussion of subjectivity and contextualization in human sciences is broad and lacks concrete evidence. The student should integrate specific studies or data to illustrate how these features influence acceptance of new evidence.

The selection of the Hitler Diaries as a case effectively anchors the discussion in a real-world example of recency bias in human sciences, illustrating how perceived authenticity can drive premature acceptance of evidence.

The transition between the two human sciences paragraphs is repetitive, reiterating previous points without advancing the argument. The student should streamline this section to focus on novel insights.

The portrayal of the natural sciences as wholly objective overlooks the theory-ladenness of observation. The student should acknowledge how paradigms influence interpretation even in experimental contexts.
Discussion of global crises is compelling but feels tacked on. The student should refine this to show how the essayâs insights about recency bias apply concretely in urgent real-world scenarios.

The analysis of the Hitler Diaries remains descriptive. The student should interrogate more deeply how authority worship and recency bias intersect in this case, and consider why experts failed to apply counter-evidence.

The paragraph ends abruptly, interrupting the logical flow. The student should complete the thought and explicitly link how methodological transparencyâor lack thereofâaffects evidence credibility in human sciences.
Reference to Comteâs positivism is presented without critique. The student should discuss limitations of positivismâsuch as its dismissal of theoretical constructsâto deepen the analysis.

The essay mistakenly reassigns the Hitler Diaries example to natural sciences, which undermines the comparative analysis. The student should maintain clear boundaries between AoKs in their case discussions.

Labeling scientific debate as a âbattlefieldâ is rhetorically vivid but oversimplifies knowledge production. The student should adopt more precise TOK terminology when describing the dynamics of scientific change.
This standalone sentence lacks context and disrupts coherence. The student should integrate it into a broader analysis, ensuring each claim clearly relates back to the central thesis.
Overall argument progression circles back on itself without developing a clear throughline of implications. Reorganizing the conclusion thematically would strengthen coherence and impact.
In-text citations vary in style. Ensure consistent formatting of authorâdate references or footnotes to maintain academic rigor and clarity.

The conclusion summarizes previous points but does not extend the analysis to novel implications or recommendations. The student should propose specific strategies for knowers to balance skepticism and openness.
The reference list shows inconsistent formatting (e.g., missing journal issue numbers). Adopt a single citation style (MLA, APA, Chicago) and apply it uniformly throughout.
All sources are secondary; there is no engagement with primary research or empirical data. The student should consult original studies or datasets to strengthen arguments with firsthand evidence.
The bibliography features a narrow range of authors. Incorporating a broader spectrum of scholarly perspectives would deepen the critical evaluation of recency bias.
Some entries lack publisher locations or DOIs. Providing complete publication details enhances traceability and credibility of cited materials.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
